evals.prompt: Build me a retro RPG-style dialog box with a character portrait, typewriter text effect, and CRT scanlines. It should look like it belongs in a SNES-era JRPG. Single HTML file.
evals.expected_output: A single HTML file with an RPG dialog box featuring pixel borders, a box-shadow character portrait, typewriter animation using steps(), CRT scanline overlay, and a retro color palette. Should use Press Start 2P or similar pixel font.

assertions.name: has-pixel-borders
assertions.note: Should not have rounded corners on UI elements (CRT frame excepted)

evals.prompt: Create a landing page for a fictional indie game called 'Dungeon Byte' in full pixel art style. Include a hero section with a pixelated logo, animated stars background, a feature list with pixel icons, and a CRT monitor frame around the whole thing. Single HTML file.
evals.expected_output: A complete landing page in a single HTML file with consistent pixel art aesthetic throughout. Should include animated elements using steps(), box-shadow pixel icons, CRT frame with scanlines and edge darkening, responsive pixel scaling using --px variable, and a cohesive retro color palette.

evals.prompt: Make a pure CSS pixel art character (like a small knight or warrior) using box-shadow, with an idle animation that cycles through 2-3 frames using steps(). Put it on a dark background with a subtle CRT effect. Single HTML file.
evals.expected_output: A single HTML file with a recognizable pixel art character built entirely from box-shadow. Should have at least 2 animation frames using steps() timing, image-rendering: pixelated if applicable, and a subtle CRT scanline/glow overlay on the background.
